title: Italian Charred Veggie Fondue
description: A twist on the classic Swiss fondue, this Italian-inspired dish incorporates charred vegetables for a smoky flavor. Perfect for a dinner party or special occasion.
instructions: Cut the eggplant into 1-inch cubes and roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until tender and lightly charred.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until caramelized and slightly charred.
In a separate skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
In a large serving bowl, combine the roasted eggplant, caramelized onions, garlic, and fondue. Stir to combine.
Cut the bread into 1-inch cubes and toast until crispy.
To serve, dip the toasted bread into the fondue and top with Parmesan cheese and parsley, if desired.
Note: If using a vegan fondue, be sure to follow the manufacturer's instructions for heating and serving.
Also, be sure to serve the fondue at a safe temperature to avoid foodborne illness.
If you're feeling adventurous, try adding some charred vegetables like bell peppers or zucchini to the fondue for extra flavor and texture.
